text:
Market today: Alphabet joining the Dow is a mild positive signal for mega-cap tech sentiment, but nothing in today's headlines is a clear catalyst for your chip/tech names. The real driver right now is your own book's weakness, not the macro tape.

Your holdings: PLTR is down 12% and flagged SELL — on margin, that hole gets deeper fast, no stop at 85 yet triggered but watch it closely. AVGO is your largest position, down 11.5%, flagged TRIM — size plus loss on margin is a double risk. PTNM is down 15% with a stop at 9.50 just below current price at 10.39 — tiny spec, could gap through your stop, manage manually.

Watch: (1) Whether AVGO stabilizes or keeps sliding — as your biggest position it drives your overall P&L most. (2) SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with limited price history — a small move against you hits hard on margin.

Bottom line: Margin amplifies every one of these red positions — trim or cut the worst offenders before they force your hand.
